A '''second battle''' occurred at [[Outpost D-34]] shortly after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]] in [[3 ABY]].

==Prelude==
{{Quote|During our attack on the Mugaari/Rebel weapons area, the Rebels launched a raid on Outpost-D34. It fell to the Rebels, despite a valiant fight.|Imperial briefing officer|<ref name="Star Wars: TIE Fighter">[[Star Wars: TIE Fighter (video game)|''Star Wars: TIE Fighter'']]</ref>}}
During the [[Battle of the Tungra sector|attack]] in the [[Tungra sector/Legends|Tungra sector]] [[Outpost D-34]] was attacked and [[Capture of Outpost D-34|captured]]. The ''[[Fogger]]'' had returned to [[Outpost D-34]] to find it in Rebel hands. ''Fogger'' immediately launched a counterattack to recapture the station.

==Battle==
The Fogger destroyed two [[Delta-class JV-7 escort shuttle|Escort shuttles]] from [[Post group|Post]]. [[Maarek Stele/Legends|Maarek Stele]] then investigated the [[Container transport|Container Transport]] [[Gopher]] to find it carrying a majority of the equipment stolen from the platform. Stele weakened the shields enough to allow [[Delta-class JV-7 escort shuttle|transport]] group [[Omega]] to board and capture the transport. From the platform Stormtrooper transport group [[Ginger group|Ginger]] then attacked the Fogger, along with Escort Shuttle group [[Claf group|Claf]], which resulted in all of them being destroyed by the Fogger and the fighter escorts. Once the defenses were eliminated. Transport [[Sigma]] then boarded and recaptured the platform, regaining full control of the system.

==Aftermath==
The Imperials were able to retake the platform and recovered much of the stolen material from the outpost in the process. Soon they would find the [[Lulsla (MC40a light cruiser)|source]] of the rebel attacks on D-34. Located in the [[Bruanii sector/Legends|Bruanii sector]].

==Behind the scenes==
This is the storyline of the mission following "medium" difficulty. If the player tries hard diffculty then more starfighter squadrons will appear to attack the Fogger.
